provisions.title: AMENDING THE LAND REGISTRATION ACT.

       MILITARY GOVERNMENT O F SANTO DOMINGO.
                 Executive Order No. 691.
             Amending the Land Registration A c t .
                       0. G. No. 3280.

    By virtue of the powers vested in the Military Government
  Santo Uomingo, the following Executive Order is hereby pro-

mulgated. :
       ARTICLE 1. The Secretary of the Supreme Court of JUS-
tice s h d i de!iver to the Secretary of the Land Court all the in-
dexes t:, the notaria! archives ser,t to and to be fcund in the for-
mer court, in sxordance with Articles 27 and 45, part second,
of the Notarial Law, and the Secretary of the Court of Appeal of

       ARTICLE 2. The Secretary of the Land Court shall org-
anize such indexes, after which they shall be kept in the archives
of said Court.
        ARTICLE 3. Such power as is granted the Superior Land
Court. by paragraph ( b ), Article 2 of Executive Order No. 647,
!z he;.+::    extended to such rezords and notarial instrument as
w x e executed betwean August 1, 1890 and December 31, 1919.
       A2:TlCLE 4. The Land Registration Act i s herehy a v e n a -
ed kty :idding the following paragraph t o Article 41 thtlrc.:)f, EIS
amended by Exezutive Order No. 590 :
        “Whenever a notary public dies. abandans his posisiton, res-
igns, is removed or bxomes disqualified, the Alcalde of the Com-
mune shaii seal the archives, first taking care that all d o c u m a t s
belonging t o the records are in their proper places. I n doing
such work, the AZcaZde shall be accompanied by his Secretary.
Three days thereafter the Alcalde and his Secretary shall proce-
ed to make a !ist of all documents of which the archive is compos-
ed, and shall deliver them t o th? carrespmding C o m e m a d o r d e
Hipotccus or R e g i s h m of Titles, as provided in the Land Regis-
tration Act.”
        This provision annuls paragraph ( b ) , Article 1 of Exec-
utive Order No. 650, of July 12, 1921, and s n e n d s the first p?ra-

        ARTICLE 5. Article 139 of the Land Registration Act,
up to the word “exsapt” in the fourth line thereof, shall read a s

      ARTICLE 6 . The ;.panish text of subparagraph ( l ) , par-
agraph ( a ) , Article 140 of the Land Registration Act, a f t e r t h e

      ARTICLE 7. Article 145 of said Law i s hereby amended
by adding at the end thereof the following paragraph :
      “Whenever in a catastral case it is, in the opinion of t h e
Judge having jurisdiction of the case, essential to t h e determin-
ation of the case to hare any paper o r document of any kind t h a t
is filed in any court of the Republic, the said Ju,dge may secure
such paper or document or the entire record in t h e case, by
means of a n order issued to the Secretary of the Court where
such d rments a r e filed.’‘
    ARTICLE 8. All 1:ws OT parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed.

                                       S. S. ROBISON,
                              Rear-Admiral, United States Navy,
                             Military Governor of Santo Domingo.
